description:
h2. Epic Goal
*As a* founder / engineering leader,
*I want* an engine that turns my git history into structured, provenanced evidence of how the software was built,
*so that* I can produce IP-provenance, R&D and competence evidence from real commit data without ever persisting source code.

h3. Scope
8-phase SEE: read-only consent + LOC/effort/cost + exec report; paginated commit ingestion + dev attribution; branch analysis + 9-type classifier + rewrite ratio; experimentation detection + R&D evidence; architecture/test evolution + module inference; evidence-graph + provenance queries; multi-regulation reports + immutable storage; session capture + competence profiling + Claude Code hooks.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/see-*.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* estimator/classifier/session suites pass.
* *Given* any analysis, *when* run, *then* source code is never persisted (audited).
* *Given* the evidence graph, *then* 5 provenance queries return results on a populated fixture.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-267…CRAN-274 (Phases A–H)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* engineering leader,
*I want* a consent model, LOC estimation, effort/cost calc and an executive report,
*so that* I get a costed, source-safe build summary the moment a repo is analysed.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/see-estimator.test.ts}}, *then* file classification + language detection + LOC + report generation are covered.
* *Given* any run, *then* source code is never persisted (audited).

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase A; productivity/complexity coefficients recalibrated (Session 52).

h3. Sources
SEE Phase A commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineering leader,
*I want* paginated, resumable commit-history ingestion with developer attribution,
*so that* even 100K+-commit repos ingest reliably and attribute work to developers.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a re-run, *then* ingestion resumes from the last cursor.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase B; Neo4j {{Developer}} + {{AUTHORED}} edges.

h3. Sources
SEE Phase B commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineering leader,
*I want* branch analysis, a 9-type commit classifier and rewrite ratios,
*so that* commit history is categorised deterministically for evidence reporting.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/see-classifier.test.ts}}, *then* all 9 types classify deterministically.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase C; long/short/merged/abandoned branches; feature/fix/refactor/docs/test/chore/spike/merge/wip.

h3. Sources
SEE Phase C commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineering leader,
*I want* experimentation detection and an R&D evidence report,
*so that* I can evidence genuine experimentation for IP-provenance / due-diligence.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the 5 algorithms, *then* each has positive + negative test cases.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase D; refactoring waves / prototype branches / rapid iteration / high rewrite / fix-after-feature; SHA-256 hashed report (reframed from R&D-tax to IP-provenance in Session 63).

h3. Sources
SEE Phase D commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineering leader,
*I want* architecture- and test-evolution detection with module inference,
*so that* I can show how the system's structure and test coverage evolved.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the analysis, *then* {{SEEModule}} nodes link to the Product/Dependency/Contributor graph.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase E; restructuring/migrations/API changes/decomposition.

h3. Sources
SEE Phase E commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* auditor / due-diligence reviewer,
*I want* an evidence graph with provenance queries,
*so that* I can trace any SEE finding back to the underlying CRANIS2 data.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a populated fixture, *then* 5 provenance queries return non-empty results.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase F; links SEE outputs to Product/Dependency/Vulnerability/Obligation; graph-summary completeness report.

h3. Sources
SEE Phase F commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* multi-regulation evidence reports in immutable storage,
*so that* I can emit CRA/NIS2/AI-Act/DORA/ISO/R&D reports that can't be altered after the fact.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* each report type, *then* it generates with content + hash.
* *Given* a written report, *then* it is immutable.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase G; report-type registry + hash chain.

h3. Sources
SEE Phase G commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineering leader,
*I want* session capture, competence profiling and Claude Code hooks,
*so that* AI-assisted work sessions are captured and profiled into competence evidence.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/see-session.test.ts}}, *then* domain detection + industry refs + competence levels are covered.
* *Given* {{tools/session-capture/setup-hooks.sh}}, *then* it generates correct hook config (PascalCase {{SessionEnd}}).

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase H; 10 competence domains; reads JSONL transcript→Markdown; Session 54 rewrite fixed event name + stdin/transcript handling (tested vs a 688-line transcript).

h3. Sources
SEE Phase H + session-capture rewrite.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* reader of CRANIS2 documentation (admin or contributor),
*I want* all documentation split into Admin and Contributor audiences with SEE + source-code guarantees,
*so that* I read only what's relevant to my role, and the source-code handling guarantees are explicit everywhere.

h3. Scope
Two-audience rewrite across EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY, CAPABILITIES-AND-SAFEGUARDS, welcome site, USER-GUIDE v4.0, FAQ v4.0, HELP-GUIDE-STANDARD v2.0, and a frontend audience filter.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* each doc, *when* read, *then* it carries the two-audience split + SEE content + source-code guarantee.
* *Given* DocsPage, *when* the filter toggles, *then* the visible TOC changes.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-293…CRAN-299

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* reader,
*I want* the executive summary rewritten with audiences + SEE + source guarantees,
*so that* the top-level doc reflects the two-audience model and SEE.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.md}}, *then* it carries the two-audience split + SEE section + source-code guarantee.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 53 + 63 ownership-rewrite commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* reader,
*I want* the capabilities & safeguards doc updated for SEE + security,
*so that* the safeguards doc documents SEE, crypto security and infra hardening.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/CRANIS2-CAPABILITIES-AND-SAFEGUARDS.md}}, *then* §2.22 (SEE), §4.1 (source handling), §4.5/4.6 (crypto + hardening) are present.

h3. Implementation Notes
Four-table store/SEE-consent/never-store/never-do layout.

h3. Sources
Session 53 + WS-documentation commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* prospective customer,
*I want* the welcome site updated with the Contributor audience + SEE,
*so that* the public site reflects both audiences and the real SEE capability.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{welcome/public/index.html}}, *then* the Contributor card + SEE/Session-Capture cards + source-code guarantee callout are present.

h3. Implementation Notes
Marketplace reframed '(Planned)'; SEE added to Pro pricing.

h3. Sources
Session 53 welcome-site commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* user,
*I want* the user guide reorganised into audience groups (v4.0),
*so that* I can find guidance by audience and the SEE features are documented.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/USER-GUIDE.md}}, *then* it carries the v4.0 header + 7 audience-tagged groups + SEE sections 41-43.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 53 user-guide rewrite.

h3. User Story
*As a* user,
*I want* the FAQ updated for SEE and contributors (v4.0),
*so that* common SEE/session-capture questions are answered and source-code answers carry the read-only guarantee.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/FAQ.md}}, *then* it carries the v4.0 header + 3 new sections.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 53 FAQ rewrite.

h3. User Story
*As a* documentation author,
*I want* the help-guide standard moved to a two-track model (v2.0),
*so that* help content maps to Admin/Contributor tracks rather than 5 roles.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/HELP-GUIDE-STANDARD.md}}, *then* it carries the v2.0 header + two-track mapping + HTML audience-tag spec.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 53 standard rewrite.

h3. User Story
*As a* user,
*I want* an audience filter in the in-app docs and help panel,
*so that* I can filter docs to my audience and the help panel routes correctly across tabs.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* DocsPage, *then* the All/Admin/Contributor toggle filters the TOC.
* *Given* a tab change, *then* HelpPanelContext re-routes on {{cranis2:tab-change}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{seo/meta-config.ts}} featureList 8→16 + SEE FAQ JSON-LD.

h3. Sources
Session 53 frontend commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* engineer maintaining the platform,
*I want* an isolated, deterministic, well-covered test stack with automated nightly runs,
*so that* tests run safely against a separate stack, flakes are minimised, coverage is high, and results are reported automatically.

h3. Scope
Isolated test stack (separate containers/DB/ports, 5-layer safety); ~300 new tests lifting route coverage to 98.5%; API-client retry on transient socket errors; deterministic IDs + rate-limit cleanup; nightly runner + Trello; E2E video-on-failure + rate-limit bypass; react-is dep fix.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{scripts/test-stack.sh start}}, *then* the isolated stack comes up with startup guards.
* *Given* the suite, *then* route coverage is 98.5% and transient-socket flakes are retried to 0.
* *Given* the nightly runner, *then* it posts results to Trello.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-300…CRAN-305 + CRAN-369 (react-is)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* an isolated test stack on separate containers, DB and ports,
*so that* tests can never touch dev/prod data.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{scripts/test-stack.sh start}}, *then* the test stack comes up; {{stop}} frees ~900MB.
* *Given* startup, *then* {{pool.ts}}/{{neo4j.ts}} guards verify URLs match {{CRANIS2_TEST_MODE}} and exit on mismatch.

h3. Implementation Notes
backend_test:3011, neo4j_test 7476/7699, {{cranis2_test}} DB; {{--profile test}} only; 5-layer safety.

h3. Sources
Session 56 isolated-test-stack commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* ~300 new tests lifting route coverage to 98.5%,
*so that* logic is well-covered and regressions are caught early.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the coverage report, *then* {{routes/}} is at 98.5% (65/66).
* *Given* the 17 test files, *then* ~300 new tests pass.

h3. Implementation Notes
SEE (209) + route-gap (~50) + admin (~30) + service unit tests; ~1,664→~1,957 total.

h3. Sources
Session 53 test-hardening commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* the test API client to retry transient socket errors,
*so that* memory-pressure flakes don't fail otherwise-green runs.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{backend/tests/helpers/api-client.ts}}, *then* retry logic is present.
* *Given* nightly runs, *then* transient-cause failures drop from 3-10 to 0.

h3. Implementation Notes
Retries {{UND_ERR_SOCKET}}/{{ECONNRESET}} ×2 with backoff.

h3. Sources
Session 53 flaky-test-fix commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* deterministic test IDs and rate-limit cleanup,
*so that* re-runs converge on the same fixtures and don't leak state.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a fresh run, *then* IDs are deterministic ({{e0000001-...}}).
* *Given* {{public-api-v1.test.ts}} afterAll, *then* billing plans restore to standard.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE}}; globalSetup seeds once + cleans rate limits; {{clean-rate-limits.ts}}.

h3. Sources
Session 53 + 56 test-helpers commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a nightly test runner posting results to Trello,
*so that* I see pass/fail every morning without running anything.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* cron, *then* {{scripts/nightly-tests.sh}} runs at 20:00 UTC.
* *Given* a run, *then* a Trello card appears the next morning.

h3. Implementation Notes
Logs with 14-day retention; Trello board {{69b076fb70d3d0cf561032b7}}.

h3. Sources
Session 58+ nightly-runner commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* E2E video on failure and an E2E rate-limit bypass,
*so that* I can debug failed E2E runs and the suite doesn't exhaust rate limits.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a failed E2E test, *then* a video artefact appears in {{e2e/test-results/}}.
* *Given* {{--profile test}}, *then* the rate-limit bypass token is recognised.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 58 visual-testing commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* react-is as an explicit dependency with a deduped lockfile,
*so that* the dependency tree is correct and reproducible.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{package.json}}, *then* {{react-is}} is an explicit dep and the lockfile is deduped.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Commit {{af2abf8}} (2026-05-07).
